title: Fluffy Indian Broccoli Biryani
description: A savory and shattering lazy-sunday Indian dish featuring purple-sprouting-broccoli, this recipe is perfect for a relaxing weekend meal. It is a no-onion-no-garlic-friendly dish wrapped in a pandan leaf vessel.
instructions: Heat ghee in a large pan over medium heat and add cumin seeds.
Add coriander powder, turmeric powder, and salt, and stir for 1 minute.
Add the basmati rice and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add 4 cups of water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and fluffy.
In a separate pan, steam the purple-sprouting-broccoli florets until tender.
To assemble the biryani, mix the cooked rice and broccoli together.
Spoon the rice and broccoli mixture onto the center of a pandan leaf, and fold the leaf to form a parcel.
Serve the biryani hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
